summary: A Go-based monolithic project utilizing an automated agent framework (GOrchestra) to extensively generate and manage test suites for a runner, schema validation, and MCP integration system.
concerns: Proliferation of agent-generated test directories (GOrchestra/glm-agents/*) polluting the repository structure
Potential redundancy between baseline tests and numerous 'extra_tests' variants
High coupling to an automated agent workflow (GOrchestra) which may obscure human-readable history
suggestions: Archive or externalize the GOrchestra/glm-agents directories to a separate artifact storage to clean up the main tree
Consolidate overlapping extra test files into the standard test suites to reduce maintenance burden
Review runner.go and handlers.go to ensure core logic remains decoupled from the auto-generated test infrastructure
